observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to propose doable and effective measures to resolve delays in liquidation such as sending of demand letters to concerned officers and employees who did not liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period to liquidate with a reminder that their salaries could be withheld should they still fail to liquidate after demand. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to avoid granting multiple cash advances, ensure sufficiency of fidelity bond, and support the grant and liquidation of cash advances with sufficient documentation.
observations.managementAction: Attached to the cash advance voucher is a signed Promissory Note to liquidate the advances within the prescribed period and failure to do so shall constitute a valid cause of withholding of salary as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 and an Authority to Deduct authorizing the cashier to deduct from the salary/or any benefits due to the officer/employee, the total amount of unliquidated cash advance. The Accountable Officer is fully aware of it because they signed and submit the 2 documents along with the Cash Advance Voucher. The Municipal Treasurer is now more strict in the implementation of the rules, liquidation in particular and assigned personnel to monitor such.
observations.reason: Per audit, there were cash advances with delays in liquidations mainly due to the payroll sheets not immediately signed by the claimants/recipients; whereas, the liquidation of cash advance for travel was attributed to the completion of the supporting documents, such as official receipts and travel documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to follow the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs under Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; for the fair presentation of the PPEs in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to formulate and adopt more aggressive collection strategies; otherwise, consider availing the remedies provided under Sections 176 and 256 of RA No. 7160 to enforce the collection and reduction of delinquent real property taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with concerned offices, to stop the practice of granting a cash advance with multiple and varying purposes, in compliance with Sections 89 and 123 of PD No. 1445 and Section 4.1.7 of COA Circular No. 97-002, to safeguard the government fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to be more assertive in notifying the recipients of the payroll to claim their pay and immediately sign the payroll sheets, and henceforth, strictly comply with Items 5.1.1 and 5.1.3 of the COA Circular No. 97-002, for the immediate liquidation of the cash advances for payroll and travel fully supported by documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and issue the ICS for semi-expendable property totaling P1,058,272.20, and to likewise prepare and maintain the SPC, RegSPI, and RSPI, for the establishment of proper control and accountability over the semi-expendable property; and
b. Municipal Accountant to maintain the SPLC to record the acquisition, description, custody, impairment, issue/transfer/disposal, repair history, and other information about the property.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the one-time cleansing of all dormant accounts following the procedures set forth by COA Circular No. 2023-008, to ascertain the validity of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to assess and require the AO to file an application for an increase in the amount of bond that is sufficient to cover her excess accountability, pursuant to Section 305 (f) of RA No. 7160 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2019, to minimize the risk that losses through defalcations, shortages, and unrelieved accountabilities might not be recovered.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that ORs are duly accomplished and must contain the nature of collections for each transaction, in compliance with Sections 111 and 123 of PD No. 1445, to enable the Audit Team to properly evaluate the appropriateness of the Municipality’s revenue classification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Health Officer to request the copy of signed and notarized MOA with the CHD 4A, pursuant to Item VI (D) (3) of the DOH and DBM JAO No. 2023-01, Article 1318 of RA No. 386 and Rule II, Section 7 of the 2004 Rules on Notarial Practice and furnish copy to the Audit Team for further verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the recognition of the authority of the Municipal Administrator and Municipal Vice Mayor to countersign the checks drawn in settlement of the Municipality’s obligations, whenever applicable, in compliance with Section 345 of RA No. 7160 and Section 43 of COA Circular No. 92-382, to ensure the system of sound internal control.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials and employees to establish control and/or monitoring system to ensure that all contracts/ POs, complete with necessary details including the contract/PO dates and conforme date of the suppliers, were submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five working days after their execution/issuance, in compliance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001, for the timely evaluation and review of the contracts/POs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) and Municipal Accountant to update, complete and maintain the property records as reference in the conduct of the one-time cleansing to ensure the existence, condition and accountability to the PPEs; and b. Inventory Committee to:  Plan/ strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period by preparing PIP containing, at the least, the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory; and  Henceforth, strictly comply with the COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition of non-existing/ missing PPE items, for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The PPE Ledger Cards, Property Cards and Property Acknowledgement Receipts were already prepared and updated. The Municipality prepared the PIP and completed the physical inventory of the PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer to ensure that projects to be implemented by phases are supported by clear delineation of work for each phase, which must be usable, and structurally sound and ensure the conduct of the detailed engineering activities for each phase.
observations.managementAction: During the year, the Municipality did not implement projects by phases, however, the Municipal Officials ensures that projects to be implemented by phases are supported by detailed engineering. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials to continually coordinate with the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) to provide necessary action, documents and other needed activities on the completion of the Tertiary School Building. The Municipality is constantly coordinating with the DPWH project team for the completion of the Tertiary School Building. Per Inspection Report dated February 14, 2025, the project is 95 percent completed and in accordance with the engineering design.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to re-check the entries and re-submit to the Audit Team the Consolidated RAAF now using the correct prescribed format and cut-off date, for January to December 2023 for further evaluation; and b. Henceforth, judiciously prepare the Consolidated RAAF at the end of the month as supported by the RAAF of each Accountable Officer (AO) and submit the same to the Audit Team monthly for verification to ensure the accurate reporting of the quantity of Accountable Forms received, issued or cancelled as of the end of the month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer reconciled and re-submitted to the Audit Team the Consolidated RAAF. The monthly Consolidated RAAF is supported by the RAAF of each AO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to maintain the SPLC to record the acquisition, description, custody, impairment, issue/transfer/disposal, repair history, and other information about the property.

observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat agreed to implement the audit recommendation. A staff will be designated to monitor all contracts and POs using a logbook or other monitoring tools, such as a whiteboard and Google Drive, to ensure daily tracking of the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRMO, in coordination with the MDRRMC, to: a. Fast track the review and finalization of the plans for the Improvement of Evacuation Center and thereafter prepare the necessary program of works and other supporting documents for the bidding process to commence; and b. Review the necessity and appropriate funding of each PPA under the Capital Outlay and STF, in compliance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121, to ensure that these PPAs will be responsive to the objectives of strengthening the capacity to build disaster-resilient communities and enhancing disaster preparedness at all levels.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD M&E Team, as created by EO No. 8, dated April 5, 2024, and other concerned officials to fully comply with the establishment of a GAD M&E System, in compliance with Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMCs No. 2013-01 and 2016-01, to ensure the effectiveness and efficiency of the Municipality’s gender mainstreaming efforts. We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to reconstitute the composition of the GAD M&E Team to include the GFPS TWG Chairperson, as required under Section 5.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and 2016-01, to ensure that the needs and interests of the public are well represented.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO and MPDC agreed to implement the audit recommendations. EO No. 2, series of 2025, dated March 3, 2025, re-organizing the composition of the GFPS was prepared and submitted to the Audit Team during the exit conference. The EO included the GFPS TWG Chairperson as member of the GAD M&E Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. MPDC, in coordination with concerned officials, to evaluate and re-assess the unimplemented PPAs if those are still responsive to the priority needs of the constituents, otherwise, reprogram the fund; and b. Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and MPDC to ensure that the appropriation for the development projects is equivalent to the 20 percent of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) and reconcile the balances presented in the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) and Utilization Report and ensure that the amounts and balances were accurately presented in their respective reports; and c. Henceforth, instruct the concerned officials to ensure that PPAs are procurement-and-implementation-ready and the conduct of Detailed Engineering activities are sufficiently carried out to optimally utilize the fund.
observations.managementAction: Assessment of the involved projects in the observation were made and presented in the management comment dated April 1, 2024 and received by the Audit Team on April 3, 2024. The appropriation for development projects was sufficiently provided in the annual appropriation. The reports were reconciled between the MBO and MPDC.
observations.reason: The utilization rate for the 20 percent DF was only 48.44 percent, leaving an unutilized balance of P24,649,291.98 as of December 31, 2024, due to inadequate procurement planning and lack of detailed engineering.

observations.managementAction: The PPE Ledger Cards, Property Cards and Property Acknowledgement Receipts were already prepared and updated.

observations.managementAction: The MGSO is now preparing the Stock Cards. Prepared and submitted the RPCI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to notify the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the project before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Engineer started to provide the project sign boards for the awarded infrastructure projects starting June 10, 2024. Inspection reports and images of signboards were provided to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMC to monitor and cause the timely implementation of the PPAs to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF and strengthen the capacity to prepare and manage disaster risks.
observations.managementAction: PPAs identified and undertaken were presented and approved by the MDRRM Council. Conducted monitoring and implemented some projects.
observations.reason: The Municipality was only able to utilize P6,603,367.90 out of P28,788,078.14 for the LDRRMF – Capital Outlay and STF due to inadequate planning, leaving a balance of P22,184,710.24.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all concerned officials exert efforts to efficiently utilize the GAD fund and ensure that the implementation of all identified PPAs be given priority in order to pursue women’s empowerment and gender equality.
observations.managementAction: The GFPS is exerting full efforts to efficiently utilize the GAD fund and ensure that the implementation of all identified PPAs is given priority in order to pursue women’s empowerment and gender equality. Implementation is pursued except for matters beyond its control or uncontrolled circumstances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to prepare and submit within the prescribed period a comprehensive list of all on-going and to be implemented government PPAs, as mandated by Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and the Municipal Engineer to notify the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken on the audit recommendation. The Office of the Municipal Engineer overlooked the submission of the notice to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: overlooked the submission of the notice to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. MGSO and Municipal Accountant to update, complete and maintain the
property records as reference in the conduct of the one-time cleansing to
ensure the existence, condition and accountability to the PPEs;

b. Inventory Committee to:
• Plan/ strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within
   the prescribed period by preparing PIP containing, at the least, the specific
   assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date and a
   schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities
   from start up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory; and
• Henceforth, strictly comply with the COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the
   guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE,
   recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition of non-existing/
   missing PPE items, for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances of
   the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Engineer and the Inspector to provide further explanation why the
project was not implemented as planned and henceforth, carefully inspect and
ensure that all projects are completely implemented as per approved Detailed
Engineering Design and Contract; and

b. Contractor to install all the missing technical items in the project area amounting to
P132,500.00, otherwise, the said amount will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and Municipal
Engineer to ensure that projects to be implemented by phases are supported by
clear delineation of work for each phase, which must be usable, and structurally
sound and ensure the conduct of the detailed engineering activities for each phase.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials to
continually coordinate with the DPWH to provide necessary action, documents and
other needed activities on the completion of the Tertiary School Building.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Re-check the entries and re-submit to the Audit Team the TMRAAF, now using
   the correct prescribed CRAAF format and cut-off date, for the months of January
   to December 2023 for further evaluation; and

b. Henceforth, judiciously and accurately prepare the CRAAF at the end of the
   month as supported by the RAAF of each AOs that handles different AFs and
   submit the same to the Audit Team monthly for verification to ensure the
   accurate reporting of the quantity of AFs received, issued or cancelled as of the
   end of the month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
propose doable and effective measures to resolve delays in liquidation such as
sending of demand letters to concerned officers and employees who did not
liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period with a reminder that their
salaries could be withheld should they still fail to liquidate after demand.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal
Accountant, to strictly enforce compliance on the granting and utilization of all cash
advances, in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO and Municipal Accountant to update, complete and maintain the property records as reference in the conduct of the one-time cleansing to ensure the existence, condition and accountability to the PPEs; We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the MGSO and Municipal Accountant, to constitute Inventory Committee that will participate in the conduct of the physical count of all property of the Municipality within the prescribed period to accomplish the inventory taking and report preparation and to adhere in the guidelines and procedures of the one-time cleansing of PPEs, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality prepared property records. The Office of the Municipal Accountant stated that they are maintaining updated and complete PPE records using the old format. For compliance, they are amenable to revise the forms and will coordinate with the MGSO and Inventory Committee. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer stated that the assigned staff under the Treasurer's Office prepares the Property Card. However, it was still ongoing due to lack of manpower and the Supply Officer I was appointed only on December 12, 2023.
observations.reason: it was still ongoing due to lack of manpower and the Supply Officer I was appointed only on December 12, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to review the budget forms and accounts for the succeeding years covering the lump-sum appropriations of Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (OMOE) and make revisions accordingly to avoid the inappropriate charging to OMOE account, in accordance with Section 2.2.4 of DBM LBC No. 2016-112; and b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the financial transactions of the Municipality are properly recorded and reported following the RCAs for LGUs to ensure the fair presentation of the affected expense accounts in the Statement of Financial Performance.
observations.managementAction: The MBO reviewed the budget forms and accounts to conform with the proper charging to the OMOE account. The transactions of the Municipality were recorded by the Municipal Accountant, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

observations.recommendation: To implement the recommendation. a. maintain SCs for each type of supplies; and b. prepare and submit the RPCI to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given date.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO prepared and submitted the SSMI for the recording of the issuances; and the MGSO started the preparation of the RPCI. The SSMI were prepared and submitted monthly to the Municipal Accountant. The SSMI were furnished by the MGSO to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of the issuances. The supplies were physically counted according to the request per/ by department. The SSMI were fully prepared and submitted monthly to the Municipal Accountant. The SSMI were prepared already on the SSMI. The SSMI were furnished by the MGSO to the Municipal Accountant. The MGSO is now preparing the Stock Cards. The preparation of RPCI were not submitted. The preparation of SCs were not fully undertaken during the year due to voluminous transactions of the Municipality.
observations.reason: voluminous transactions of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant the relevant documents pertaining to the completion of the projects as basis for the reclassification of the completed projects into its proper PPE accounts; and b. Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to transfer under the respective assets the completed projects under the CIP accounts to the respective assets accounts and to record the corresponding provision for depreciation including its prior year's depreciation, in compliance with Section 50, Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Paragraphs 4 and 65 of IPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: The relevant documents such as Statement of Work Accomplished, Inspection and Completion Report and Billing from Contractors of the seven completed projects were forwarded by the Office of the Municipal Engineer to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the reclassification of the CIP accounts into its proper PPE accounts. The Municipal Accountant provided copies of JEVs reclassifying the completed projects into the respective assets under the CIP accounts and the accounts to its proper accounts. Depreciation were already computed and adjusted in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectable for the year which will serve as basis to draw a JEV in recording the RPT Receivable/SET Receivable and credit to Deferred RPT Income/Deferred SET Income; and b. Reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables accounts with the Municipal Assessor's records based on the updated market values and the assessed value of the properties under the Municipality's jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant was furnished by the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible as of July 31, 2023 and ongoing negotiation with the service provider for the purchase of Real Property and Tax Assessment system that will link the assessment records from the Office of the Municipal Assessor to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer that will help reconcile the records between the offices. The Certified Statement of RPT/SET Delinquencies as of December 31, 2023 was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Concerned Municipal Officials to prepare and sign the AIR based on the delivered items inspected and accepted; and thereafter, forward original copies of Delivery Receipts (DRs), IARs and other delivery documents to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of the delivered goods; and b. Municipal Accountant to draw JEV by debiting Due from NGAs and crediting Prior Period Adjustment account in the same amount of P473,383.79 to account for the remaining ewallet balance with the PS-DBM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant secured from the Office of the Municipal Mayor copy of the documents for the ewallet balance through letter dated August 18, 2023 and showed remaining balance of P116,129.48 which were then recorded in the books through JEV No. 101-2023-08-001811 dated August 29, 2023. The balance were then fully consumed as of year-end and adjusted in the books through JEV No. 101-2023-12-003412 dated December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV by debiting the AP account in the amount of P1,733,000.56 and crediting the Due to Officers and Employees and Due to LGUs account in the amount of P1,681,000.56 and P52,000.00, respectively, to ensure that accounts are accurately presented in the financial statements; and henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 in the recording of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reclassified the accounts through JEV Nos. 101-2023-03-000350 and 101-2023-03-000351 dated March 15, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Draw JEV by debiting AP account and crediting Prior Period Adjustment account in the same amount of JEV No. 101-2023-03-000353 dated March 15, 2023 to correct the balance of the accounts; and implement the audit recommendation. b. Record only as AP those goods and services which were actually delivered, received and accepted as of the cut-off period, pursuant to Section 4(s), Chapter 1 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 101-2023-03-000353 dated March 15, 2023 was prepared to correct the accounts. Recorded AP as of year-end were supported by actual deliveries and/or services rendered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to exert extra effort to locate the documents and information on the details of the various fund transfers from different NGAs amounting to P817,742.87 and/or make a representation on the process and return of the unused balances to the SAs, in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 and Section 111 of PD No. 1445, otherwise, remit the said amount to the Bureau of the Treasury (BTr).
observations.managementAction: The balance of P9,520.00 from the Philippine Coconut Authority as share of the Municipality for the repair and rehabilitation of damaged roads were reclassified to Due to Government-owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs) Account. While, the P808,222.87 were returned to the BTr through DV No. 100-2024-05-0548 dated May 15, 2024 and deposited on May 22, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor to: a. Continue the coordination with the Provincial Assessor on the preparation and submission of the Schedule of Fair Market Values (SFMVs) of land, buildings and other structures; and b. Reevaluate or reassess the appraised values of the properties under the Municipality's jurisdiction based on the prevailing market rates and thereafter make necessary revisions every three years, pursuant to Sections 212 and 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Assessor sent communication letters to the Office of the Provincial Assessor for coordination on the conduct of the general revision of real property assessments. The Municipal Assessor attended the meeting on August 11, 2023 for the finalization of the proposed SFMVs for agricultural lands with the Office of the Provincial Assessor and submitted to the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) Region IV-A the proposed SFMVs of the Province of Laguna for review and approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (LDRRMP) of the Municipality by focusing on the PPAs requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and impact on the DRRM; and b. Monitor and cause the timely implementation of the PPAs to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF and to strengthen its capacity to prepare and manage disaster risks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality prepared the LDRRMP by focusing on the PPAs requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and impact on the DRRM. The Municipality able to utilize only P3,368,432.80 or 26.28 percent out of P12,819,249.28 appropriation for the 70 percent Mitigation Fund (MF) due to postponement of the construction of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) building and the installation of related equipment, furniture and fixtures therein, availment of services and products at a lower price, non-availability of suppliers with complete documents and the non-exigency on the purchase of additional emergency supplies, tools and equipment due to availability and/or existence of remaining supplies from the previous years.
observations.reason: postponement of the construction of the MDRRMO building; availment of services and products at a lower price; non-availability of suppliers with complete documents; non-exigency on the purchase due to availability of remaining supplies from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMC to annually review and update the LDRRMFIP including all the PPAs planned for the previous years from the unexpended balances of Quick Response Fund (QRF) and Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRM) - Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE).
observations.managementAction: The revised LDRRMFIP was prepared and submitted on January 25, 2024 showing the allocation for the current and unexpended previous years' balances.

observations.managementAction: The list of in-kind donations (Sagip Kapamilya) and recipients were compiled/ consolidated and submitted by the MSWDO to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to strictly observe the timeliness in the preparation and submission of the approved GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team, pursuant to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 and Section 4.1.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The GPB was endorsed by DILG per Certificate of Review and Endorsement dated November 14, 2022 which were submitted to the Audit Team. While the GAD-AR was uploaded to DILG Portal on January 11, 2024 and submitted to the Audit Team on January 18, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable property following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality proceeded with the disposal of unserviceable PPEs of the Municipality. The last disposal was made on December 19, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require an annual physical inventory of all supplies and materials of the Municipality to be conducted by a committee consisting of representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, MGSO and Supply Accountable Officer of the department or office concerned.
observations.managementAction: The annual physical count of supplies and materials were conducted by a committee consisting of representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, MGSO and Supply Accountable Officer of the department or office concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal drivers to prepare a Monthly Report of Official Travels summarizing in chronological order their trips for the month and designate an officer to prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for various types of motor vehicles showing the total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle to facilitate the validation of the reasonableness of gasoline consumption pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: The Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption were implemented starting July 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMC to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP by including the PPAs planned for the previous years from the unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all concerned officials to exert efforts to efficiently utilize the GAD fund and ensure that the implementation of all identified PPAs be given priority in order to pursue women's empowerment and gender equality.
observations.managementAction: The GFPS prioritized the implementation of identified PPAs to pursue women’s empowerment and gender equality. The Municipality was able to utilize 80.65 percent of the total GAD appropriation.
observations.reason: underutilization was due to transition period and non-attainment of participant target numbers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to submit to the Audit Team the LDRRMFIP covering all COVID-19 related programs, projects and activities to avoid suspension of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The Special Trust Fund (STF) of the Municipality was still not included in the submitted LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Executive Committee and GAD Technical Working Group to provide technical assistance to the Local Planning and Development Offices (LPDO) to establish and maintain GAD Database and ensure that it is operational. Likewise, provide the funds necessary for its establishment.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: GAD Executive Committee and GAD Technical Working Group was not able to provide GAD Database because they diverted their attention to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of
unserviceable property following the procedures set forth in COA Circular
No. 92-386; and
b) Municipal Accountant to drop from the books the disposed unserviceable
properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a) require an annual physical inventory of all the supplies and materials of the
Municipality to be conducted by a committee consisting of representative
from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, Municipal GSO and Supply
Accountable Officer of the department or office concerned;
b) require the GSO to submit to the Municipal Accountant the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and distribution report within five
days after the end of each month; and
c) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that SSMI, distribution reports
and other pertinent documents were attached to the Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) in recording issuances of supplies and materials and regularly
conduct reconciliation of inventory records with GSO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer
and all concerned department heads to meticulously and judiciously prepare
the Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with Section 7 of the Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184 and ensure that all necessary information required in the APP
and its amendments, if any, are reflected therein to serve as a planning and
control tool for efficient procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal drivers to
prepare a Monthly Report of Official Travels summarizing in chronological
order their trips for the month and designate an officer to prepare the Monthly
Report of Fuel Consumption for various types of motor vehicles showing the
total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for
each vehicle to facilitate the validation of the reasonableness of gasoline
consumption of the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council to review and update annually the
LDRRMF Investment Plan by including the programs/projects/activities (PPAs)
planned for the previous years unexpended balances of Quick Response Fund
(QRF) and DRRMF- Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. properly prepare, maintain and update periodically the required Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each class of PPE, listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, useful life and other necessary information; and
b. draw Journal Entry Voucher to drop the small value property items from the books by debiting Prior Years’ Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation of the respective PPE, and by crediting the respective PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of PPE are maintained and updated periodically.
Drawing of JEVs relative to dropping of small value properties were implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accounting and Property Department to:
a. reconcile the parcels of land per Municipal Assessor’s Office records as against the Accounting Office records; 
b. retrieve all available documents pertaining to unrecorded parcels of land to evaluate the ownership and the details of acquisition; and
c. record the parcels of land at their acquisition cost, however, if the land were acquired through non-exchange transaction, determine when they were donated to the Municipality and record the land at their fair value at the time of donation. In determining the fair value, seek the assistance of the Provincial Appraisal Committee to properly appraise the value of land.
observations.managementAction:
The records of Assessor’s Office as against the Accounting Office records were reconciled.
All Tax Declarations in the name of the Municipal Government of Nagcarlan were retrieved, checked and verified by the Assessor’s Office to validate ownership excluding those DepEd properties and those identified as belongings to Barangay.
The Municipal Accountant assured that they will record the newly acquired land at their acquisition cost.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Accountant to ensure that expenditures of the Municipality are properly classified in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and
b. the Municipal Budget Officer to review the budget for the succeeding years covering the lump-sum appropriations of OMOE and make revision of the budget in accordance with the results of the review following the account description mentioned in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction:
All the payments for maintenance and other operating expenses were properly recorded to their appropriate expense account.
Revision of the budget for the succeeding years covering the lump-sum appropriations of OMOE and results were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Office Orders or any equivalent document from the Municipal Mayor showing that the personnel/workers who claimed COVID-19 hazard pay were authorized to report for work during ECQ and Modified ECQ.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has recovered and had submitted the Office Orders. The Municipal Accountant undertake to see to it that all disbursement vouchers are supported by complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting document on the payment of materials amounting to P1,588,619.54 and justifications for allowing the payment thereof despite incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has recovered and had submitted the lacking supporting documents. The Municipal Accountant undertake to see to it that all disbursement vouchers are supported by complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Officer to prepare 1) Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-Kind Donations; 2) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods; and 3) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated PPE; and submit the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten (10) working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Officer submitted the required quarterly reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to:
a. prepare and submit within the prescribed period a comprehensive list of all on-going and to be implemented government projects as mandated by Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; 
b. prepare and maintain a monthly monitoring report containing all the necessary details and information of all the Project Status and in preparation of the quarterly reports as required under Section 3.2 of the COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
c. notify the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer submitted the comprehensive list of all on-going and to be implemented government PPAs within the prescribed period.
Municipal Engineer submitted their monthly monitoring report containing all the necessary details and report to the COA office.
Copies of Notices of Award were submitted to COA office within ten days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure optimal utilization and timely implementation of the 20 percent development fund (DF) and direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) in consultation with the barangay officials and constituents to identify priority developmental projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF and ensure that only implementable PPAs during the year are included in the AIP.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality utilized the 20 percent Fund by implementing the projects that were not completed in the previous year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMC to: a. review and update annually the LDRRMFIP by including the Programs/Plans/Activities (PPAs) planned for the previous years' unexpended balances of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and DRRMF-Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) transferred to the Special Trust Fund and to the PPAs for DRRMF Capital Outlay (CO); and b. ensure that the submitted LDRRMFIP has signatures of the person responsible for its preparation and approving officer.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO prepared the LDRRMF Investment Plan and the LDRRMC approved the LDRRMFIP. The Municipal Disaster Risk Management Officer prepared the LDRRMFIP and the LDRRMC approved it. The LDRRMFIP was submitted and duly signed.
observations.reason: The LDRRMF Investment Plan prepared by the LDRRMO did not include the Programs/Plans/Activities (PPAs) planned for the previous years’ unexpended balances. Also, copy of the LDRRMF Investment Plan was not submitted to COA office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Executive Committee and GAD Technical Working Group to provide technical assistance to the Local Planning and Development Offices (LPDO) to establish and maintain GAD Database and ensure that it is operational. Likewise, provide the funds necessary for its establishment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) asked technical assistance for the establishment of GAD Database. The MSWDO asked technical assistance and requested technical office equipment for the procurement of computers.
observations.reason: GAD database was not established due to lack of funds and trained personnel. The MSWDO prioritized the construction of their office building and procurement of computers.

observations.managementAction: Reconciliation Drawing of JEVS relative to reclassification of PPEs were implemented re: JEV No. 100-2020-08-001746 to 001748 dated August 20,2020 and JEV No. 200-2020-08-00325 dated August 20, 2020. observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry to correct the recording of disbursements by debiting the appropriate expense or asset accounts upon liquidation of the fund and debiting Due to NGAs upon liquidation of the fund in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2015-09.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made the adjusting entry to correct the recording of disbursements funded by the National Government. The recommendation was fully implemented and now being applied in the recording of disbursements funded by the National Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to judiciously assess the probability of collection of loans receivable; b. the Municipal Accountant to set-up allowance for impairment in accordance with IPSAS 29 and/or set-up request for write-off of receivables, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 for the amounts which are no longer collectible; and c. the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of loans receivable and designate key personnel to strictly monitor the loans granted and enforce the payment thereof and institute any legal actions against the defaulting debtors.
observations.managementAction: The Management will start the process of request for write-off for the dormant (10 years or more current) loan receivables and related interest receivable until December 31, 2009. The Accounting Department already prepared JEV for the set-up of Prior Years Allowance for Impairment both for the Loans Receivable -Others and Interests Receivable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide the users of the financial statements additional information value as provided by the Budget Officer in Annex D.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, details of the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses is already included in the Notes to Financial Statement Annex D.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. the Municipal Accountant to follow the guidelines set forth in IPSAS 12 and Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs for the proper recognition of the inventories and expenses; and b. the Inventory Custodian to properly account and monitor the receipts and issuances of the supplies in accordance with Sections 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer/GSO and Municipal Treasurer started the preparation of supplies ledger cards and recognized the inventory in the books in the JEV preparations. The GSO /Municipal Treasurer/staff started the preparation of stock balances of medicines, medical and laboratory supplies as well as other supplies and started the inventory based on record of balances of medicines, medical supplies as well as other supplies and provided information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure optimal utilization and timely implementation of the programs/projects/ activities (PPAs) funded under the 20 percent DF and direct: a. the Municipal Engineer to prepare Program of Works of all the priority PPAs under the 20 percent DF which will be the basis of appropriation for each project in the Annual Investment Program and Annual Budget; and b. the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), in consultation with the barangay officials and constituents to identify priority developmental projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF and ensure that only implementable PPAs during the year are included in the AIP.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC coordinated with the barangay officials in identifying priority developmental projects. The MPDC coordinated with the Municipal Engineering Office for Program of Works. Most of the PPAs reported in the 20 percent DF were supported by Program of Works.
observations.reason: Most of the planned projects were not implemented because documentary requirements are still under process while the other projects were subject for realignment. The MPDO coordinated with the barangay officials in identifying priority projects but not all planned projects were implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to continue to exert best efforts to fully comply with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 by strictly enforcing the segregation of waste at source to reduce the volume of waste collected; and provide necessary financial and technical assistance to the 12 barangays to facilitate the establishment of Material Recovery Facilities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor ensured the regular collection of segregated waste in the whole municipality and provided financial and technical assistance in the establishment of MRF in the 12 barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF to strengthen its capacity to prepare and manage disaster risks and conduct close monitoring of the full implementation of the planned programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO utilized the LDRRMF by implementing the following projects: a. New rescue vehicle was purchased; b. Evacuation center was improved; c. Drainage canal in Poblacion 2 was improved; d. Allotted budget to PPE’s in previous years was used due to COVID 19 Pandemic; e. New generator set purchased; and f. Construction of Multipurpose MDRRMO Building.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Officer-in-Charge, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (OIC-MSWDO) to formulate policies on the grant of Aids to Individuals in Crisis Situation (AICS) particularly on the frequency and the amount to be provided for each type of financial assistance with reference with Department of Social Welfare and Development Memorandum Circular (DSWD MC) No. 11 series of 2019; b. request Sangguniang Bayan to review and pass the policies formulated by the OIC-MSWDO, through a Municipal Resolution, that would serve as the official guidelines in the granting of medical, burial and educational assistance in order to promote uniformity in identifying the qualified beneficiaries and the amount to be granted; and c. require the OIC-MSWDO to establish database and monitoring mechanism in the grant of financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (OIC-MSWDO) already submitted the copy of the approved policies on the grant of AICS. The Sangguniang Bayan passed Resolution No. 2021-050 adopting Memorandum Circular No. 11, Series of 2019 of the DSWD on Revised Guidelines on the Implementation of AICS. The establishment of database and monitoring mechanism in the grant of financial assistance is still on-going. The MSWDO prioritized the programs or projects related to the COVID-19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Budget Officer to include in the annual budget, sufficient fund necessary for the premiums of all insurable property of the Municipality in compliance with the Property Insurance Law and COA Circular No. 92-390. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of insurable properties so that necessary insurance coverage will be applied with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Inventory of the Property was submitted to Commission on Audit last February 18, 2021 for year ended 2020. The Budget Officer already included the necessary funds for premiums of insurable property of the Municipality. The Municipal Treasurer facilitated the preparation and submission of the list of insurable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. the Municipal Budget Officer to request additional funding from the Sangguniang Bayan to facilitate the renovation of the Pasalubong Center; and b. the BPLO-Designate and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to monitor the implementation and ensure the immediate operation of the Pasalubong Center.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer requested additional funding from the Sangguniang Bayan. The BPLO - Designate and Municipal Planning and Development Staff coordinated with the Municipal Engineering Office for the preparation of sketch plan and Program of Works for the remaining scope of work for the completion of the Pasalubong Center. The remaining scope of work for completion is yet to be done in order to start its operation.
observations.reason: The remaining scope of work for the completion of the Pasalubong Center is yet to be done in order to start its operation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Business Permit and Licensing Officer to: a. enforce collection of unpaid rentals and uncollectible receivables; b. strictly observe the pertinent provisions in the Treasurer's Office to issue Notice on the regular payment of monthly rentals and imposition of penalties to delinquent fixed stall owners; and c. ensure execution/renewal of contract yearly.

Circular No. 2016-005 for the amounts which are no longer collectible; and
c. the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of loans receivable and
designate key personnel to strictly monitor the loans granted and enforce
payment thereof and institute any legal actions against the defaulting
debtors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial Statements to
provide the users of the financial statements of additional information value
for decision-making purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Accountant to follow the guidelines set forth in IPSAS 12 and
Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs for the proper recognition of the
inventories and expenses; and
b. the Inventory Custodian to properly account and monitor the receipts and
issuances of the supplies in accordance with Sections 114 of the MNGAS
for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure optimal utilization and
timely implementation of the programs/projects/activities (PPAs) funded under
the 20 percent development fund (DF) and direct:
a. the Municipal Engineer to prepare Program of Works of all the priority PPAs
under the 20 percent DF which will be the basis of appropriation for each
project in the Annual Investment Program and Annual Budget; and
b. the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) in consultation
with the barangay officials and constituents to identify priority
developmental projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF and ensure
that only implementable PPAs during the year are included in the AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Environment
and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to continue to exert best efforts to
fully comply with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 by
strictly enforcing the segregation of waste at source to reduce the volume of
waste collected; and provide necessary financial and technical assistance to
the 12 barangays to facilitate the establishment of Material Recovery Facilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF
to strengthen its capacity to prepare and manage disaster risks and conduct
close monitoring of the full implementation of the planned programs, projects
and activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council to:
a. review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan by including the
Programs/Plans/Activities (PPAs) planned for the previous years'
unexpended balances of Quick Response Fund (QRF) and DRRMF-
Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) transferred to the
Special Trust Fund and the PPAs for DRRM- Capital Outlay (CO); and
b. ensure that the submitted LDRRMFIP has signatures of the person
responsible for its preparation and of the approving officer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Officer-in-Charge, Municipal Social Welfare and Development
Office (OIC-MSWDO) to formulate policies on the grant of Aids to
Individuals in Crisis Situation (AICS) particularly on the frequency and the
amount to be provided for each type of financial assistance with reference
with Department of Social Welfare and Development Memorandum Circular
(DSWD MC) N0. 11 series of 2019;
b. request Sangguniang Bayan to review and pass the policies formulated by
the OIC-MSWDO, through a Municipal Resolution, that would serve as the
official guidelines in the granting of medical, burial and educational
assistance in order to promote uniformity in identifying the qualified
beneficiaries and the amount to be granted; and
c. require the OIC-MSWDO to establish database and monitoring mechanism
in the grant of financial assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Budget Officer to include
in the annual budget, sufficient fund necessary for the premiums of all
insurable property of the Municipality in compliance with the Property
Insurance Law and COA Circular No. 92-390.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer
to facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of insurable properties
so that necessary insurance coverage will be applied with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Budget Officer to request additional funding from the
Sangguniang Bayan to facilitate the renovation of the Pasalubong Center;
and
b. the BPLO-Designate and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer
to monitor the implementation and ensure the immediate operation of the
Pasalubong Center.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Business Permit and Licensing Officer to:
a. enforce collection of unpaid rentals and surcharges from delinquent
stallholders, otherwise, cancel the contract of lease to avoid further loss
from uncollectible receivables;
b. strictly observe the pertinent provisions in the Municipal Revenue Code on
the regular payment of monthly rentals and imposition of penalties and
sanctions for late payment; and
c. ensure execution/renewal of contract yearly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Executive
Committee and GAD Technical Working Group to provide technical assistance
to the Local Planning and Development Offices (LPDO) to establish and
maintain GAD Database and ensure that it is operational. Likewise, provide the
funds necessary for its establishment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer/ General Services Officer to prepare the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and dispose the said property in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445, after which draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the disposal of the unserviceable property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor remind the Municipal Treasurer that the grant of 10% discounts on prompt payment of RPT and SET Receivables is applicable only once from January 1 to March 31 of each year except when payment of taxes was made on installment basis. However, in installment, once the taxpayer failed to pay on time for the first quarter, interest will be charged to the whole amount of tax due and discounts for the next three quarters will not be applicable even if payments were made on time. Also, interest on late payment shall start from January, thus, late payment for the months of April, May and July should be charged with interest at 2% per month multiply by the number of month in arrears or by 4 months (January, February, March and April) in case of April, 5 months in case of May, 7 months in case of July and so forth.
observations.managementAction: Late payments of real property taxes were charged with interest for the full amount due.
